name: AI Company Frontend Libraries
description: Minimalistic frontend libraries suited for a modern AI company UI

libraries.name: Tailwind CSS
libraries.description: Utility-first CSS. No opinions about components, total control over every pixel. Ideal as the base layer.

libraries.name: clsx + tailwind-merge
libraries.description: Conditional class merging without conflicts. Essential when building reusable components on top of Tailwind.

libraries.name: Open Props
libraries.description: Curated CSS custom properties. Consistent spacing, type scales, and color tokens in pure CSS, no framework needed.

libraries.name: Radix UI
libraries.description: Unstyled, accessible primitives. Dialogs, dropdowns, tooltips — behavior without aesthetic opinions. Bring your own styles.

libraries.name: shadcn/ui
libraries.description: Copy-paste components built on Radix + Tailwind. Owned, not installed — modify freely without fighting a library.

libraries.name: Ark UI
libraries.description: Headless component primitives from the Chakra team. Framework-agnostic, clean API, less opinionated than Radix.

libraries.name: cmdk
libraries.description: Command menu primitive. The ⌘K modal that users of AI tools expect. Minimal, composable, zero styles.

libraries.name: Motion (Framer)
libraries.description: The gold standard for React animation. Layout animations, exit transitions, gesture handling — fluid and declarative.

libraries.name: AutoAnimate
libraries.description: One-line list/DOM animations. Add, remove, reorder with automatic transitions. Near-zero setup, works anywhere.

libraries.name: Geist
libraries.description: Vercel's typeface. Clean, technical, slightly humanist. Designed for developer-facing products. Sans + Mono included.

libraries.name: Lucide
libraries.description: Consistent, minimal icon set. 1500+ icons at a single stroke width. Clean SVG, tree-shakeable, active maintenance.

libraries.name: Phosphor Icons
libraries.description: Six weights, 1300+ icons. More expressive than Lucide when you need thin or bold variants for hierarchy.

libraries.name: Recharts
libraries.description: Composable charting on D3. Simple API, decent defaults, customizable enough for dashboards without fighting the lib.

libraries.name: React Hook Form
libraries.description: Performance-first form state with minimal re-renders. Pairs cleanly with Zod for schema validation.

libraries.name: Zod
libraries.description: TypeScript-first schema validation. Type inference from schema definitions. Critical when parsing LLM output safely.
